signals.idea: Implement first-class shot selection and structured timeline context for the video editor agent: single-click shot-group selection, agent payloads with shot and clip timeline metadata, reliable same-shot semantics, and a duplicate/edit/place-after-selected workflow with tests.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline summary context: the plan assumes backend timeline config already carries shot names, but `pinnedShotGroups` only stores `shotId`/`trackId`/`clipIds`/`mode`, so `describeTimeline()` cannot emit shot names without an additional shot lookup or a widened input contract.

Selected-clip payload plumbing: Recurring debt: the plan updates some payload types but misses the stored attachment path, so `shot_id`/`shot_name` would be dropped by AgentChat send mapping and by attachment persistence/recovery across turns.

Same-shot semantics: the plan only applies direct `shot_id` to `duplicate_generation`, but shot resolution for travel defaults, active references, task shot reuse, and video-travel LoRAs still goes through `findShotForGenerations()`, so the feature remains only partially first-class.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 5.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items.criterion: describeTimeline() output includes a 'Shot groups:' section listing each pinned shot group with shotId, shotName, trackId, clipIds, and mode when pinnedShotGroups exist in config
Each clip line in describeTimeline() includes shot=<shotName> when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group
SelectedClipPayload accepts optional shot_id and shot_name fields without breaking existing consumers
buildSelectedClipsPrompt() includes shot info in selected clip lines when shot_id/shot_name are present
Clicking a shot group label in the timeline selects all clips in that group
Frontend agent invocation payload includes shot_id and shot_name for clips that belong to pinned shot groups
executeDuplicateGeneration() uses shot_id from selectedClips when available, skipping the findShotForGenerations DB lookup
All existing tests in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/ continue to pass
New unit tests cover: describeTimeline with/without shot groups, buildSelectedClipsPrompt with shot context, duplicate-generation with shot_id from payload
New frontend test covers shot group click-to-select in TimelineCanvas
System prompt mentions shot context guidance for the LLM
Manual smoke test: select shot group → send agent message → verify agent response references the shot context

unresolved_flags.evidence: Checked /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts and /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts; shot names are resolved from separate `shots` data, not from timeline config.
